name: github_corpus_convergence
host_notes: Phase B-2 no-GraphSearch baseline on the trellis-ai PR corpus — keyword + semantic only. Mirrors the Phase B-1 baseline file (phase_b1_2026-05-07_baseline_no_graphsearch.json) so the with-vs-without comparison is symmetric across both corpora.

skills_unlocked_by_graphsearch.cross_pr_lineage: 0.0 → 1.0 (forward wasInformedBy traversal — PR #66 surfaces its 5 cited PRs)
skills_unlocked_by_graphsearch.topic_content: 0.963 → 1.0 (small lift; semantic was already strong on rich PR bodies)

skills_regressed_by_graphsearch.adr_landing: 1.0 → 0.0 — surprising regression. Without GraphSearch, the 3 ADR PRs (#3, #4, #5) rank highly on keyword+semantic alone. With GraphSearch, expanding from seeds (#3 etc.) pulls in adjacent ADR PRs like #16 (graph ontology) which crowd out the requested set under the 8-item budget.

skills_unchanged_at_zero.multi_pr_series: Both 0% — no entity numbers in the intents (e.g. 'Phase 1 through Phase 4 PRs that shipped scenarios 5.1, 5.2, 5.3'), so seed extractor has nothing to seed GraphSearch with.
skills_unchanged_at_zero.author_attribution: Both 0% — same root cause: the dependabot author entity_id isn't matched by 'authored by' / 'not by ronsse' phrasing.

graphsearch_ab_diff.headline: GraphSearch is a strong net positive for lineage queries (+1.0 on two skills) but exhibits a real regression on adr_landing — pack-budget contention from over-fetched neighbors. The fix is per-query budget or strategy-aware ranking, not turning GraphSearch off.

decision_recorded: Phase B-2 baseline pairs cleanly with the with-GraphSearch run from earlier today. The diff documents the actual contribution of seeded GraphSearch on this corpus: lineage skills go from 0% to 100%, but adr_landing regresses from 100% to 0% under pack-budget contention. This makes the per-query pack budget follow-up more concrete — it's not just unblocking failing skills, it's fixing a real GraphSearch regression too.
follow_ups_pending: Per-query pack budget — restoring adr_landing to 100% under GraphSearch is now a concrete win measurable against this baseline.
Phrase-based seed extraction for non-entity intents (multi_pr_series, author_attribution) — neither corpus moves on these without it.
